mission.title: jaeger: Multiarch docker images
mission.description: Multiarch docker images. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current jaeger deployment
steps.description:
Verify your jaeger version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n observability -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger
helm list -n observability 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working jaeger installation.

steps.title: Review jaeger configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant jaeger configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n observability -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger
kubectl get configmap -n observability -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=jaeger
```
## Requirement - what kind of business use case are you trying to solve?
Ability to utilize multiarch images

## Problem - what in Jaeger blocks you from solving the requirement?
I have a rpi k3s (arm64) cluster that I use to run a web app written

steps.title: Apply the fix for Multiarch docker images
steps.description:
## Which problem is this PR solving?
- first step to support #2292 

## Short description of the changes
- `TARGETARCH` is the `ARG` need for `buildx` https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/builder/#automatic-platform-args-in-the-global-scope

now could build local by

```bash
make build-ui
make
```yaml
make build-ui
make build-query GOOS=linux GOARCH=arm64
make build-all-in-one GOOS=linux GOARCH=arm64
make build-query GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64
make build-all-in-one GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64
repo=jaegertracing/all-in-one # all change to custom repo
docker buildx build --platform=linux/amd64,linux/arm64 --push -f cmd/all-in-one/Dockerfile -t $repo:latest cmd/all-in-one
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n observability -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger
kubectl get events -n observability --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Multiarch docker images" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
## Which problem is this PR solving?
- first step to support #2292 

## Short description of the changes
- `TARGETARCH` is the `ARG` need for `buildx` https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/builder/#automatic-platform-args-in-the-global-scope

now could build local by

```bash
make build-ui
make build-query GOOS=linux GOARCH=arm64
make build-all-in-one GOOS=linux GOARCH=arm64
make build-query
codeSnippets:
make build-ui
make build-query GOOS=linux GOARCH=arm64
make build-all-in-one GOOS=linux GOARCH=arm64
make build-query GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64
make build-all-in-one GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64
repo=jaegertracing/all-in-one # all change to custom repo
docker buildx build --platform=linux/amd64,linux/arm64 --push -f cmd/all-in-one/Dockerfile -t $repo:latest cmd/all-in-one

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with jaeger installed or the issue environment reproducible.
